answer:
We first find the equivalent impedance and resistance referred to the high-voltage side.  
From the short-circuit test:  
\( I_{sc} = 6.25 \, \text{A} \), \( V_{sc} = 131 \, \text{V} \), \( P_{sc} = 214 \, \text{W} \).  

\[
Z_{eq} = \frac{131}{6.25} = 20.96 \, \Omega, \quad
R_{eq} = \frac{214}{6.25^2} = 5.4784 \, \Omega, \quad
X_{eq} = \sqrt{20.96^2 - 5.4784^2} \approx 20.24 \, \Omega.
\]

Rated current on HV side: \( I_{\text{rated}} = \frac{15000}{2400} = 6.25 \, \text{A} \).
